signature.instructions:
Analyze two product records from different sources to determine if they represent the exact same real-world product.

Focus on the core product identity rather than superficial string overlap. Be highly vigilant of 'near-miss' pairs: two records are only a match if they refer to the exact same product configuration. A different model, version, edition (e.g., Standard vs. Premium), platform (e.g., Mac vs. PC), or upgrade/upsell path means they are different products, even if the titles look highly similar. 

Additionally, account for formatting inconsistencies: product titles may be noisy (containing SKUs or seller names) and manufacturer fields may be missing or inconsistent across sources. Infer the true product details from the available evidence.

Provide a concise justification detailing your comparison, especially noting any version or variant differences. Finally, output a boolean `match` value (True if the same product, False otherwise) and a calibrated `match_probability` score between 0.0 and 1.0 (where 1.0 is absolute certainty of a match, and 0.0 is absolute certainty of a non-match).

fields.description: True if the two records are the same entity
Probability in [0, 1] they are the same
